alkylation reagent: 15 mM iodoacetamide (Sigma-Aldrich) in the dark for 30 min to alkylate cysteine groups
alkylation concentration: 15 mM iodoacetamide (Sigma-Aldrich) in the dark for 30 min to alkylate cysteine groups
cleavage agent: trypsin (2 μg) was directly added to the samples and allowed to digest for 18 h
enrichment method: To enrich global phosphopeptides at each period, TiO2-affinity chromatography was first performed
fractionation method: Pooled peptide samples were fractionated using a Pierce™ High pH Reversed-Phase Peptide Fractionation Kit
fragmentation method: the data-dependent mode producing ten of the most abundant ions from the full scans was fragmented in the HCD mode
instrument: an LTQ-Orbitrap Velos mass spectrometer in positive ion mode
ionization type: The electrospray voltage was set to 2.0 kV for the TMT-labeled sample analysis
labeling: labeling was performed with 6-plex TMT for relative quantification at each period
reduction reagent: 5 mM dithiothreitol (Sigma-Aldrich) was added for cysteine residue reduction
reduction concentration: 5 mM dithiothreitol (Sigma-Aldrich) was added for cysteine residue reduction
